summary: Frontend-only polish iteration verified. All 5 user asks pass: no letterbox on media (figure ≈ media height, diff=2px = 1px border on each side); every 'Bracket' occurrence on About Me (bio + Currently panel) and in the Bracket case study H1 is an <a data-testid='bracket-link' href='https://use-bracket.com/' target='_blank'>; other case study H1s (Tello, Hyundai, Harman) are NOT wrapped; responsive layouts render correctly at 1024/1280/1440/1920 and mobile 390 with proper scroll vs viewport-lock behavior (xl breakpoint); micro-interactions (chip hover amber-invert + icon-wobble, HOVER TO INSPECT label, .hover-lift + .boot-glow on stat tiles, .press on buttons, case-list pl-5 slide + chevron translate) all confirmed via computed style checks. Regression items (crab avatar, Ctrl+K palette, Open Bracket CTA, contact form, all 4 case studies rendering media) all pass.
critical_code_review_comments: Work.jsx paired-media grid uses default grid-item stretch (align-items: normal → stretch). When two adjacent figures in a row have media with different aspect ratios (e.g., Verizon IMG 2.05:1 next to a video with different ratio), the shorter figure stretches to the row height, creating dark empty space inside that figure (letterbox-like). Not observable during headless testing because H.264 videos don't decode (browser fallback 300x150), but in real browsers with decoded videos this can still show a gap between media bottom and figure bottom if aspect ratios differ within a row. Consider `align-items: start` on the grid, or wrap each figure in a self-start item so figures hug media exactly. Currently only guaranteed hug when paired media share the same aspect ratio (Bracket/Harman).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All iteration-5 user asks verified. Media hug: figure diff = 2px consistently, which is 1px border × 2. Bracket links: 3 total on the site (2 on AboutMe + 1 in Bracket H1). Ctrl+K opens command palette (not Meta+K in headless Linux). Videos won't decode in headless — measure DOM/testids/classes rather than videoWidth for future runs. Grid-stretch caveat in Work.jsx paired-media may show letterbox-like gap only when paired media have differing aspect ratios; user's explicit ask is met per current image/video content.
